title: Blepharoglossum crenulatum
intro: Blepharoglossum crenulatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as a type of fern. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in treating various ailments including wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Blepharoglossum crenulatum include flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Blepharoglossum crenulatum is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This species belongs to the genus Blepharoglossum, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. The classification of Blepharoglossum crenulatum places it within the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns. Additionally, it is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. At the kingdom level, Blepharoglossum crenulatum is classified as Plantae.
